MT-Q2.Question: azxtrvyGiven that A = {1, 2, 3, 4} and B= {1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11}, list A ∩ B.

MT-Q4.Question: czxtrvyWhat is the next number in the sequence 128, 64, 32, 16, ...?

MT-Q5.Question: azxtrvyA grade seven learner got 16 marks out of 20 in Mathematics. Convert the marks to a percentage.

MT-Q7.Question: czxtrvyWhat are the prime factors of 15?
MT-Q7.e: Numbers that you multiply to get  the number

MT-Q8.Question: bzxtrvyFind the perimeter of a triangular field measuring 125 metres by 100 metres by 75

MT-Q9.Question: bzxtrvyA watermelon was cut into 7 equal pieces. If four pieces were eaten, what fraction of the watermelon were the remaining pieces?

MT-Q11.Question: czxtrvyIn Hindu-Arabic numerals, the Roman numeral LXXIV can be written as ...

MT-Q12.Question: dzxtrvyFind the mode of 5, 8, 6, 5, 7, 6, 5 and 7.
MT-Q12.e: Mode is the most repeated number

MT-Q13.Question: dzxtrvyA trader bought cooking oil at K63.00 and sold it at K49.00. What was his loss?
MT-Q13.e: Loss is the amount you lose when you sell at a lower price than the price you paid for an item

MT-Q14.Question: azxtrvyChange ⅘ to a decimal fraction.
MT-Q14.e: ⅘ is the same as ⁸⁄₁₀. A fraction does not change when you multiply the numerator and the denominator by the same number

MT-Q15.Question: dzxtrvyExpress 6 X 6 X 6 X 6 X 6 in index form.

MT-Q16.Question: czxtrvyExpress the ratio 8 metres to 800 metres in its lowest terms.
MT-Q16.e: Simply divide the numbers by their highest common factor (8)

MT-Q17.Question: czxtrvyConvert 0.25 to a percentage.
MT-Q17.e: Percent means out of hundred. So 25 out of 100 is 25/100 or 0.25 or 25%

MT-Q18.Question: dzxtrvyWhich of the following numbers are factors of 12?
MT-Q18.e: Factors are whole numbers  which do not leave a remainder when the original number is divided by those whole numbers

MT-Q20.Question: dzxtrvyWhich of the following statements is correct?

MT-Q21.Question: azxtrvyIn a certain year, Mrs Chongo produced 10,000 bags of rice while Mrs Mubita produced 9,874 bags of rice. Calculate the difference between the number of bags of rice produced.

MT-Q22.Question: czxtrvyArrange the Roman numerals below from the smallest to the biggest. DCV, LXV, CDV
MT-Q22.e: DCV is 605, LXV is 65, CDV is 405

MT-Q24.Question: bzxtrvybus takes 2 hours to cover a distance of 96km. What is its average speed?
MT-Q24.e: divide distance by hours taken. Speed is distance covered in given time

MT-Q25.Question: bzxtrvyFind ⁴⁄₉ of 36.
MT-Q25.e: of means multiply. This is the same as ⁴⁄₉ x 36. So, Multiply the numerator x 36 and then divide by denominator, or divide 36 by denominator and multiply by the numerator

MT-Q26.Question: azxtrvyIf 7 packets of salt cost K49.00, what will be the cost of 13 packets of the same type?
MT-Q26.e: First find the price of each packet by dividing the given cost by the given quantity

MT-Q28.Question: azxtrvyK25,000.00 was invested for 2 years at the rate of 4% per year. Calculate the simple interest.
MT-Q28.e: Interest every year is <sup>4</sup>/<sub>100</sub> x 25,000 = 1000. So for to years it will be K2000

MT-Q29.Question: bzxtrvyConvert 342 to base ten.
MT-Q29.e: Work from lefet to right. (2 x 1) + (4 x5) + (3 x 5 x 5)= 2 + 20 + 75

MT-Q30.Question: czxtrvyWhat is the average of 20°C, 38°C, 28°C, 32°C and 22°C?
MT-Q30.e: Add the temperatures and divide them by how many they are (20+38+28+32+22) divided by 5

MT-Q31.Question: czxtrvyThe mass of a piece of beef is 15.64kg. Express this mass to 1 decimal place.
MT-Q31.e: The nearest number on the right side of the position for first decmal place is 4 and it is less than 5, so drop the number. If it was 5 or greater you would increase the 6 by one to give 15.7

MT-Q32.Question: bzxtrvyEvaluate 5³.
MT-Q32.e: Power means multiply the number by itself by the given number of times. 5 x 5 x 5 (Three times)

MT-Q33.Question: azxtrvy8 boys can paint a hall in 6 hours. How many hours can 12 boys take to paint the same hall, if they work at the same rate?
MT-Q33.e: Find the total hours of work required to complete the work (6 x 8=48hrs) then divide by the new number of boys (48 divided by 12 gives 4)

MT-Q34.Question: azxtrvyFind the value of 12 x (15 +5).
MT-Q34.e: Use BODMAS. Calulate what is in the brackets before multiplying

MT-Q35.Question: czxtrvy105 people used a bus for a trip. If the bus carried 35 people only at a time, how many trips did it make?
MT-Q35.e: divide total number of people by the number of people that the bus carries

MT-Q37.Question: bzxtrvyMumbi bought a dress at K400.00. She later sold it at K500.00. Calculate her percentage profit.
MT-Q37.e: Profit is  selling price  minus buying price. Percentage profit is profit divided by buying price x 100

MT-Q38.Question: azxtrvyThe shape of a water tank at Mrs Bupe’s farm is that of a cuboid. If its length is 14m, breadth 12m and height 10m, find the volume of the water tank.
MT-Q38.e: volume is height x width x length

MT-Q39.Question: azxtrvy A string which is 22.5m long is cut into smaller pieces each measuring 2.25m. How many pieces of such length can be cut from the string?
MT-Q39.e: divide the string by the lengthof each piece

MT-Q40.Question: czxtrvyRound off 24,178 to the nearest hundred.
MT-Q40.e: The nearest number on the right side of the position for hundreds is larger than 4 so increase the number in the position of hundreds by one

MT-Q42.Question: bzxtrvyExpress 18 as a product of prime factors.
MT-Q42.e: A prime factor is a number that can only be divided by 1 and itself

MT-Q43.Question: czxtrvy-3 + (+12) =
MT-Q43.e: +(+) is the same as +, so this is the same as -3+12 or +12-3 or simply 12-3

MT-Q44.Question: dzxtrvyGiven that $1 is equal to K6.00, what is the price in Zambian Kwacha of a car costing $2,125?

MT-Q45.Question: azxtrvySolve the inequation 4x + 2 > 10.
MT-Q45.e: Subtract two from each side then divide each side by 4. Whatever is done on one side of the sign must be done to the other side

MT-Q46.Question: bzxtrvyIf set V = {1, 2, 3}, how many subsets does it have?
MT-Q46.e: The empty set and the full set and different combinations of the elements are all subsets. {},{1},{2},{3},{1,2},{1,3},{2,3},{1,2,3}

MD-Q47.Question: bzxtrvyMutale had K3,000.00 to spend. She bought two dresses at K500.00, two belts at K90.00, a handbag at K250.00 and a pair of shoes at K499.00. How much money did she remain with?
MD-Q47.e: subtract the total expenses from the total amount of money Mutale had.
